title: Write-Off and Recovery
event: Reyes Co. writes off a customer's $900 account as uncollectible. A month later, the customer pays in full. Prepare all entries.

lines.label: cushion used
lines.why: The write-off spends allowance that was expensed in advance.
lines.trap: NO Bad Debt Expense here — the expense already happened at estimation time.

lines.label: account removed
lines.why: The specific customer comes off the books.
lines.trap: Net realizable value doesn't move — AR and the allowance drop together.

lines.label: claim revived
lines.why: The account returns — it proved collectible.
lines.trap: Reinstate BEFORE collecting so the customer's history shows the recovery.

lines.label: cushion restored
lines.trap: Not revenue, not a gain — the estimate simply un-spends itself.

lines.label: claim settled
lines.trap: Across all three entries, income effect = zero — everything ran through balance-sheet accounts.

memorize.body: Under the allowance method, write-offs and recoveries never touch income — the expense lives entirely in the year-end estimate.
Direct write-off (Dr Bad Debt Expense at failure) exists but violates matching — GAAP allows it only when amounts are immaterial.

questions.prompt: Bad debt EXPENSE recorded at the moment of the $900 write-off = ?

distractors.feedback: The expense was estimated in advance. The write-off just consumes existing allowance.

build.scaffold: Write off: allowance vs AR. Recover: reverse, then collect. Income never moves.
